refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined the entire load→travel→dump cycle into a single requirement, which includes multiple distinct phases. To be atomic and solution-neutral, the FR is split into sub-FRs each addressing one phase of the cycle. This refinement focuses on the load phase as one atomic functional requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load phase under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, ensuring the p95 cycle time is less than or equal to the customer-defined target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination
Adjust equipment operational parameters to meet cycle time
Implement real-time monitoring and adaptive control of load phase
Enhance operator training and procedural adherence for load phase efficiency
selected_dp.selected_dp: Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination

potential_sources: Interdependence between load phase and travel/dump phases timing
Influence of external site conditions on load phase duration
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP focuses on logical sequencing and coordination within the load phase, minimizing direct coupling with other phases. External factors are acknowledged but managed separately.

assumptions_risks: Cycle time target is achievable through process optimization alone
Site conditions and operational policies remain stable and do not introduce unpredictable delays
Operator behavior and training can be standardized to support process sequencing
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive control mechanisms or real-time monitoring as secondary DPs to handle variability in site conditions and operational execution.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing and optimizing operational processes including load phase sequencing and coordination

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational data and sends status updates relevant to load phase optimization

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends commands and coordination tasks to other modules or subsystems during load phase

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with external systems and modules to coordinate load phase activities

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as load phase sequencing is a core operational process; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle module handles physical aspects; sequencing logic would be partially contained but requires significant diffusion to other modules.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control; load phase optimization only partially relevant, leading to high diffusion.
Safety Services focus on safety alerts and monitoring; load phase sequencing is outside primary scope, causing poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP 'Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination' because it directly manages operational processes and can encapsulate the sequencing logic with minimal diffusion to other modules.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if physical constraints affect sequencing logic.
Coordination with Teleoperation may require careful interface design to avoid coupling.
Changes in load phase process requirements may require updates across multiple modules if interfaces are not well defined.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination' directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase by focusing on process sequencing and coordination, which are key factors influencing cycle time. The DP logically satisfies the FR as it targets the operational process improvements needed to meet the cycle time acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if physical constraints affect sequencing logic.
Coordination with Teleoperation may require careful interface design to avoid coupling.
Changes in load phase process requirements may require updates across multiple modules if interfaces are not well defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating strong cohesion and minimal scattering of the DP. It is the most appropriate IM boundary to encapsulate the DP, managing sequencing logic centrally and minimizing diffusion risks. However, some diffusion risks remain due to necessary interactions with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, which should be managed via well-defined interfaces.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by measuring the cycle time p95 against the CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions, as specified in the acceptance criteria. The NGHE Solution module's interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) support data collection and command dissemination needed for verification.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and appropriate, as it is responsible for managing and optimizing operational processes including load phase sequencing and coordination, aligning well with the FR.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to manage diffusion risks and ensure modular independence.
Include monitoring and feedback mechanisms within NGHE Solution to detect and adapt to physical constraints from Vehicle module affecting sequencing logic.
Document update procedures and responsibilities for changes in load phase process requirements to minimize cross-module impact and maintain interface stability.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirement with a logically sound design parameter and a well-contained implementation module. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and diffusion risks are manageable through interface definitions and monitoring mechanisms. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to maintain the current decomposition level and focus on implementing the recommended interface contracts and monitoring strategies rather than further decomposing the FR at this stage.

revise.why: To explicitly define interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, include monitoring and feedback mechanisms to handle physical constraints, and document update procedures to manage cross-module impacts, thereby reducing diffusion risks and ensuring modular independence.

assumptions: The DP 'Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination' fully captures the necessary operational improvements to meet cycle time targets.
The NGHE Solution module is capable of encapsulating the DP with high containment and low diffusion as indicated by scores.
Interface definitions and monitoring mechanisms can effectively manage the identified diffusion risks.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if physical constraints affect sequencing logic.
Coordination with Teleoperation module may introduce coupling if interfaces are not well defined.
Changes in load phase process requirements may impact multiple modules if update procedures are not maintained.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing cycle time p95 meets CT_target under specified conditions.
Documentation of interface contracts and monitoring mechanisms between NGHE Solution, Vehicle, and Teleoperation modules.
Records of update procedures and responsibilities for load phase process changes.
